title: Tapping for feeling stuck
subtitle: A five-minute tapping sequence for the inertia that keeps you in place
excerpt: A five-minute tapping sequence for the feeling of being stuck. When you know something needs to change but you can't seem to move. When the gap between where you are and where you want to be has stopped feeling like motivation and started feeling like proof.

practice.whenToUse: When you feel paralysed by a decision or a life area that needs movement, or when low-level stuckness has been present for long enough to feel like your default state

content.text: Feeling stuck is usually about fear, not lack of ability. The movement is possible. The cost of moving feels too uncertain. This sequence works with the fear of movement rather than the question of where to go.
Setup
Tap the karate chop point and repeat three times:
Even though I feel stuck and I don't know how to move, I accept myself and where I am right now.
Even though this stuck feeling has been here for a long time, I am willing to let something shift.
Even though I can't see the path forward yet, I accept myself in this moment.
Round one: name it
Tap through the points (eyebrow, side of eye, under eye, under nose, chin, collarbone, under arm, top of head) while saying:
This stuck feeling.
The weight of not moving.
The fear of what moving might cost me.
The fear of getting it wrong if I try.
The way staying still feels safer than going somewhere I might regret.
Round two: shift
Movement is possible.
I don't need the whole path. Just one step.
I have moved before when I didn't know how.
Being stuck is not the same as being broken.
Something in me knows what the next move is.
I am ready to let it become clearer.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education.
